title: Refactor LSP-006 (LSP): Subclass changes the behavior of assignment to delete keys on None value

detected_issue.description: Subclass changes the behavior of assignment to delete keys on None value
detected_issue.reasoning: In a standard MutableMapping, assigning a value to a key (jar[key] = value) is expected to store that value. RequestsCookieJar overrides this behavior so that assigning None actually deletes the key. This unexpected side effect violates the postconditions of the base interface contract.

fix_logic.summary: Remove the side-effect of deleting cookies when assigning None in __setitem__
fix_logic.rationale: The Liskov Substitution Principle is violated when a subclass changes the behavior of a standard interface method (MutableMapping.__setitem__) to perform a deletion instead of a store. By removing the implicit deletion on None, we restore the expected behavior of the mapping interface. Users should use 'del jar[name]' or the explicit 'remove_cookie_by_name' function for deletion.

changes.search_text:
def __setitem__(self, name, value):
        """Dict-like __setitem__ for compatibility with client code. Throws
        exception if there is already a cookie of that name in the jar. In that
        case, use the more explicit set() method instead.
        """
        self.set(name, value)
changes.replace_text:
def __setitem__(self, name, value):
        """Dict-like __setitem__ for compatibility with client code. Throws
        exception if there is already a cookie of that name in the jar. In that
        case, use the more explicit set() method instead.
        """
        if value is None:
            raise ValueError("RequestsCookieJar does not support deleting cookies via __setitem__ with None. Use del jar[name] instead.")
        self.set(name, value)
